# Questions in Livestream 189

Live Stream #189: The Thirteenthest of Fridays

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=1127" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:47</a> | What software do you use for guitar composition? | He said he uses Reaper as his DAW and MuseScore for composition and playback work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=1195" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:55</a> | What did you think of the Billy Carson vs. Wes Huff debate? | He described it as badly one-sided, with Huff clearly better prepared and Carson's efforts to suppress clips only making the loss look worse. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=1743" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">29:03</a> | Will David ever write a short book that is not heavy? | He joked about the wording, then said their novel had gone to beta readers and should be a manageable paperback. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=2141" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:41</a> | Is the extent of Egyptian imperial evidence in Canaan really only two garrisons and two administrative complexes? | He said no; those were the major sites, but there were also smaller outposts and garrisons. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=2447" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">40:47</a> | Would some Canaanite areas welcome and work with Egypt for defense? | He said some would, but Egyptian protection was unreliable; Egypt mainly cared that tribute kept flowing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=2581" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:01</a> | What were the perks of being pharaoh versus vizier versus nomarch, and which would you rather be? | He said pharaoh was the best position, with taxation, gifts, military power, and the top place in a corrupt patronage hierarchy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=2855" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:35</a> | How does the power of pharaoh compare to the power of modern Egyptian presidents? | He said both systems are military dictatorships in some respects, though pharaohs and modern presidents are constrained in different ways. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=3032" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:32</a> | What historical inaccuracies are in Tobit? | He said he had not read Tobit closely enough for that, but its theological problems, such as fish parts driving out demons and almsgiving saving from death, were enough to trouble him. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=3596" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:56</a> | What do the numbers 7, 10, 12, and 40 mean in the ancient Near East? | He said 7 is associated with holiness or the divine, while other numbers need context and are not all simple fixed symbols. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=4011" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:51</a> | Who conquered and enslaved the Israelites in Avaris? | He identified Ahmose I as the king and Ahmose son of Abana as one of the generals involved. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=4073" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:53</a> | Why do we not hear more about the ancient Hittite and Amorite empires if they were so great? | He said the Amorites were a confederacy rather than an empire and left limited records, while the Hittites were great but received less popular attention than Egypt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=4298" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:38</a> | Why is Tobias burning fish parts in Tobit not the same thing as Moses putting lamb's blood on Israelite doorways? | He distinguished the two by saying Tobit treats power as residing in the fish parts, while Passover blood functions as an act of faith and obedience to God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=4472" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:32</a> | If Ahmose was such a popular name, why did it take so long to get an Ahmose II? | He said Amenhotep became the more popular royal name, though Ahmose remained common among non-royal people. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=4843" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:43</a> | What clothes and shoes do you recommend for Egypt? | He recommended modest, heat-appropriate clothing, especially stricter coverage for women, and comfortable footwear such as sneakers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=5692" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:52</a> | What do you think of John Walton's ideas about Genesis and animals surviving a world full of water? | He said the exact Walton idea was unclear, but he reads Genesis 1-11 as setting up sacred-space and ritual categories rather than concordist science. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=6018" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:18</a> | What series would you recommend on evidence for the resurrection? | He did not know a specific series to recommend, but favored a maximal-facts approach over minimal-facts or presuppositional approaches. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=6116" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:56</a> | What biblical scholars would you recommend checking out? | He named scholars such as Brent Strawn and Lydia McGrew, while warning that no scholar should be followed uncritically on everything. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=6647" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:47</a> | Why are there two images on the Kuntillet Ajrud inscription, with one figure interpreted as male in one and female in another? | He said the inscription is controversial, the drawings are not clearly tied to the text, and he does not see the middle figure as female. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=8148" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:48</a> | Why do kids have to teach adults biblical basics? | He said biblical literacy among adults is very poor because many have forgotten or never learned the basics, leaving children sometimes better prepared. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=8945" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:05</a> | How can Protestants hold the Nicene Creed if ecumenical councils are fallible? | He said sola scriptura still allows consulting fallible sources; Protestants accept the creed insofar as it agrees with Scripture. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=10220" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:50:20</a> | How do you answer someone who says cultural context can excuse discarding anything in the Bible? | He said that is not real contextual reading; everyone reads with some context, so the issue is whether one uses the ancient context or a modern one. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=10699" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:58:19</a> | How did the KJV-only movement start? | He traced it to late nineteenth-century reactions against critical Greek texts and newer English translations, amplified by guilt-by-association arguments. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=11931" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:18:51</a> | What are the ramifications of spiritual practices like yoga and Ouija boards? | He distinguished ordinary secular stretching from spiritually oriented yoga, warned against occult forms, and said Ouija boards should be avoided. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=12604" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:30:04</a> | How should Christians approach slavery in the Bible? | He said slavery is descriptive rather than prescriptive in the Bible, with different forms regulated and progressively restricted rather than endorsed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=12955" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:35:55</a> | Is the "I am" statement in John 8:58 a direct proclamation related to Exodus 3:14? | He said yes, and argued the surrounding reaction shows the audience understood it as a major divine claim. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=13532" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:45:32</a> | What are your thoughts on the doctrine of original sin? | He said there are multiple versions, and he favors a federal-headship or representative-authority account rather than a genetic-contagion model. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=14733" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:05:33</a> | Was there backlash from the recent popcorn review of Frank Turek's Exodus-evidence video? | He said no major backlash had appeared, perhaps because the Billy Carson debate had overshadowed it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/CQdYUSe3hV4?t=15855" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:24:15</a> | Where did Egyptians think dreams came from, especially good, bad, or foretelling dreams? | He said they generally thought dreams came from the gods, who could send omens or knowledge of the future. |
